sourceMovie.title: Detective Conan: The Private Eyes' Requiem
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Detective Conan: The Private Eyes' Requiem (2006) as a animation theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The animation and mystery blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Case Closed: The Fourteenth Target
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Detective Conan: The Private Eyes' Requiem clicked for you, Case Closed: The Fourteenth Target (1998) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and mystery, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Nancy Drew
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Detective Conan: The Private Eyes' Requiem clicked for you, Nancy Drew (2007) is a strong next stop: both trade in mystery and adventure, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Detective Conan: The Private Eyes' Requiem?
faq.answer: Try Case Closed: The Fourteenth Target, Nancy Drew, Tekkonkinkreet—each shares DNA with Detective Conan: The Private Eyes' Requiem while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Detective Conan: The Private Eyes' Requiem?
faq.answer: Detective Conan: The Private Eyes' Requiem (2006) is best described as Animation, Mystery, Adventure. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Detective Conan: The Private Eyes' Requiem part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Detective Conan Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
